title: Pass Chain Record
description: Governed traceability record for a complete multi-pass reasoning chain execution.

chain_id.description: Deterministic identifier for this chain execution, derived from task_type, context_id, and chain config.

task_type.description: AI task type that triggered this chain (e.g., 'meeting_minutes').

context_id.description: context_id of the context bundle used as input to this chain.

routing_table_version.description: Version of the routing table resolved at chain build time. Null if routing is not version-pinned.

pass_sequence.description: Ordered list of pass specs defining the planned execution sequence.

pass_results.description: Ordered list of pass result records, one per executed pass. Each item conforms to pass_result.schema.json.

intermediate_artifact_refs.description: References to all intermediate artifacts produced during chain execution.

status.description: Final status of the chain. 'terminated' = circuit breaker halted execution; 'escalated' = circuit breaker halted and escalation is required; 'completed' = all passes ran to completion.

completed_at.description: Timestamp when chain execution finished. Null if still in progress.

circuit_breaker_policy.description: Policy that governs circuit-breaker behaviour for this chain.

max_passes.description: Maximum number of passes allowed in the chain.

max_failed_passes.description: Maximum total failed passes before the chain is terminated.

consecutive_failure_limit.description: Maximum number of consecutive failed passes before the chain is terminated.

persistent_validation_failure_limit.description: Maximum number of schema validation failures across the chain before escalation.

escalation_policy.description: Governs whether circuit-breaker termination triggers an escalation flag.

termination_reason.description: Human-readable reason why the chain was terminated early. Null for successfully completed chains.

escalation_required.description: True when the chain has been escalated due to persistent failures or policy violations.

warnings.description: Non-fatal warnings raised during chain execution.
